ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Continued To Nov-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alden Knisbacher for (administrators) Keva Knox & Shvaz Knox.

Mr. Knisbacher reported that they are still having difficulty regarding who gets what and if a trust is to be established, that it is hard to get his clients together and to come to an agreement. The Court stated that it would like to take the matters off calendar and the parties can refile when they are ready. Mr. Knisbacher asked for a continuance so that he has leverage with his clients. The Accounting Petition and the Petition for Allowance of Extraordinary Compensation was continued to November 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that this will be the last continuance and that if the parties are not ready the petitions will go off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Sep-19-2016 Continued To Nov-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-22-2016 Continued To Sep-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alden Knisbacher for Keva Knox & Shvaz Knox.

The Court asked counsel what the game plan is. Mr. Knisbacher stated that they had issues getting an agreement on distribution, that they now have a plan, that there was an issue regarding the non pro rata distribution, that they would like to get court approval on it, that they will need to set up a trust for the beneficiary who is on MediCal, that they can submit the non pro rata distribution within a week and that they will need to hire an attorney to do the trust. The Court stated that it thinks it will be easier to amend the petition, that once amended the other two petitions can go off calendar and requested that counsel provide the non pro rata distribution plan. Mr. Knisbacher stated that he will need two weeks to file the amended petition. The accounting petition and the petition for allowance of extraordinary compensation were continued to September 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alden Knisbacher for Keva Knox & Shavaz Knox.

Mr. Knisbacher reported that they are close, that they need to finalize the 2014 taxes and that they need a couple more weeks. The Court stated that it had understood that they were very close at the last hearing. Mr. Knisbacher stated that he has been ill, that he is in touch with the clients and is urging them to get it done. The status on filing a petition for final distribution or a report on administration was continued to April 19, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and that if it is not done by that date we will need a new petition for approval and that sanctions or suspension will be considered.

Off Calendar Feb-16-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Lou Williams specially appearing for Alden Knisbacher for (co-administrators) Keva Knox & Shavaz Knox.

Ms. Williams stated that they will need an additional 60 days, that Mr. Knisbacher had a family emergency, that he will comply with the examiner's notes by the end of February and that he will get the waivers filed. The Court stated that the pending report of administration said that this would close in November, that that time has long gone by, that the pending report on status of administration will be taken off calendar and that a new status date of March 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 is set for a new filing of a petition for final distribution or a report on administration.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Continued To Feb-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alden Knisbacher for petitioner Shavaz Knox

The Court stated that the petitioner needed to get a petition for final distribution filed. Mr. Knisbacher stated that the pending action in Alameda was close to resolution, and that he expected a dismissal of that action fo be filed in January. He stated that once the action was dismissed, the final distribution plan would be finalized and the petition should be on file within three months. The Court asked if a six-week continuance would be sufficient, and Mr. Knisbacher acknowledged that it would. The Court stated that he also needed counsel to submit a proposed order approving the status of administration. Mr. Knisbacher replied that he will submit one to the Court. The matter was continued to February 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not reported)
